# Cosmic Dance of Interdependence
## Scripture
"Two are better than one, because they have a good return for their labor: If either of them falls down, one can help the other up. But pity anyone who falls and has no one to help them up." - Ecclesiastes 4:9-10

## Reflection
As we gaze up at the night sky, we're reminded of the intricate web of relationships within our universe. The planets orbit the sun, the stars cluster in galaxies, and the galaxies are connected in a vast cosmic network. This dance of interdependence is a reflection of God's design for human relationships. Just as the celestial bodies rely on each other for stability and harmony, we too are created to thrive in community. In our faith journey, we're not alone; we're part of a larger body of believers, supporting and uplifting one another.

## Prayer
Dear Heavenly Father, thank you for the beautiful reminder of interdependence in your creation. Help us to appreciate the value of community and relationships in our lives. As we navigate the challenges and joys of our faith journey, may we be mindful of the need to support and encourage one another. May our lives be a reflection of your harmonious design, and may we shine brightly together as a testament to your love and grace. Amen.
